LungeR.

George, by the Grace of God, of the United Kingdom of

Great Britain and Ireland and of the British Dominions beyond the seas king, Defender of the Faith, Emperor of India, do., dc., sc., Io ail and singular to whom these Presents shall come, Greeting!

Whereas, for the better treating of and arranging certain matters which are now in discussion, or which may come into discussion, between Us and other Powers and States in connexion with the forthcoming Peace Congress We have Judged

It expedient to invest a fit person with Full Power to conduct the said discussion on our part; Know ye, therefore, that we, reposing especial Irust and Confidence in the Wisdom, Loyalty,

Diligence, and Circunspection of Our Right Irusty and wellbeloved Cousin and Councillor Alfred Viscount Milner, Knight

Grand Gross of Our Most Honourable Order of the Bath, Knight

Grand Cross of Our Most Distinguished Order of St. Michael and st. George, etc., etc,, our Secretary of State for the Colonies have named, made, constituted and appointed, as We do by these

Presents name, make, constitute and appoint him Our undoubted

Gormissioner, Procurator, and Plenipotentiary; Elving to him all manner of Power and Authority to treat, adjust, and conclude with such Ministers, Cormiasioners or Plenipotentiaries as may be vested with similar Power and Authority on the part of any

Powers or States as aforesald, any Treaties, Conventions or

Agreements that may tend to the attainment of the above-mentioned end, and to sign for Us, and in our name, everything so agreed

upon

upon and concluded, and to do and transact all such other matters as may appertain thereto.

in as ample manner and form,

and with equal force and efficacy, as We Ourgelves could do, if personally present; Engaging and Pronising, upon Our Royal

Word, that whatever things shall be so transacted and concluded by Our said Commissioner, Procurator, and Plenipotentiary, shall, subject if necessary to our Approval and Ratification, be agreed to, acknowledged and accepted by Us in the fullest manner, and that We will never suffer, eithet in the whole or in part, any person whatsoever to: infringe the same, or act contrary thereto, as far as It Iles in our power.

In witness whereof We have caused the Great Seal of our

United Kingdom of Great Britain and Ireland to be affixed to these Presents, which we have signed with Our Royal Hand.

Given at our Court of st. Janes, the first day of January in the Year of our Lord, One Ihousand Nine hundred and Nineteen and in the Ninth Year of Our Reign.
